philosophy: Clean, confident, adaptable. A universal modern aesthetic that works across contexts — from tech keynotes to academic lectures to business reports. Precision without coldness, clarity without sterility. The design language of effective communication, regardless of culture or industry.
core_principles: Clarity is universal — every audience understands clean hierarchy
Restraint signals confidence — less is more across all contexts
Typography does the heavy lifting — clear type hierarchy transcends boundaries
Space creates importance — what you leave out matters more than what you put in
Motion serves comprehension — every animation must earn its place
Color is purposeful — accent colors have jobs, not just presence
Adapt to content, not to fashion — the aesthetic serves the message
forbidden_patterns: Decorative particles, lens flares, excessive glow trails
Rapid cuts driven by beat detection rather than narrative
Texture overlays that add visual noise without meaning
Overloaded frames — more than 3 focal elements competing simultaneously
parent_traditions: Dieter Rams' 10 principles of good design
